title: Magical Flaky Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A winey and flaky Algerian dish featuring red pepper flakes and a sweet glaze, this recipe is perfect for a special occasion and is Buddhist-temple-friendly as it does not contain any animal by-products or strong spices that may be offensive.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: boneless and cut into 2 inch pieces

instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the lamb to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion and garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped apricots, honey, and apple cider vinegar to the pot, and stir to combine.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the tagine for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
